result:
**Goal: Add a Spin Ice & Emergent Magnetic Monopoles mode.**

A 2D frustrated magnet simulation on a square-ice lattice where arrow-spins obey "ice rules" (2-in/2-out at each vertex). Violations of these rules behave as emergent magnetic monopole quasiparticles — they nucleate as monopole-antimonopole pairs connected by Dirac strings, diffuse through the lattice as a Coulomb gas, and annihilate on contact. This sits in the Physics & Waves category and is scientifically distinct from the existing Ising Model (which has no frustration or emergent fractionalized excitations).

**Presets:** Equilibrium Ice (thermal fluctuations at ice-rule ground state), Monopole Gas (high-temperature Coulomb plasma of monopoles), Field Quench (sudden applied field drives monopole avalanche), Dirac Strings (track string tension between monopole pairs), Kagome Ice (triangular frustrated variant), Pauling Entropy (measure residual entropy of ice manifold).

**Why this:** It's cutting-edge condensed matter physics (spin ice discovered ~1997, emergent monopoles confirmed ~2008), visually rich on a discrete lattice (colored arrows + glowing monopole particles + string connections), and fills a niche between the existing Ising Model and Magnetic Field Lines modes without overlapping either.

Shall I build it?
